skill.name: expert-interview-generator
skill.description: Generates a full expert interview article including introduction, Q&A body, and summary based on interview questions and expert background. Use when you have interview questions and an expert profile and need a polished article.
skill.summary: Generates a structured expert interview article from interview questions and an expert background profile using a guided multi-step workflow.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 55 static analysis findings are false positives. The 43 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections are markdown code block examples showing bash commands, not executable code. The 5 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections match on YAML front matter fields and non-cryptographic text in prompt files. The 4 'System reconnaissance' detections match workflow documentation describing inputs and parameters. The 2 'Hardcoded URL' detections reference the legitimate source repository. The skill is a legitimate content generation tool with a simple Python article assembly helper and prompt references.

low_findings.title: External commands in documentation code blocks
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains 43 markdown code blocks with bash command examples (e.g., python scripts/flow.py). These are documentation examples, not executable code in the skill's runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All 43 instances are inside markdown fenced code blocks showing example usage, not active shell execution.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded source repository URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 7 contains a hardcoded URL to the GitHub source repository. This is a standard attribution link with no security risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a legitimate source code repository attribution, not a data exfiltration endpoint.

content.user_title: Generate polished expert interview articles from Q&A inputs
content.value_statement: Writing a professional expert interview article requires careful structure and consistent formatting across multiple sections. This skill automates the creation of a complete interview article with introduction, Q&A body, preface, and summary using a guided multi-step workflow.
actual_capabilities: Generates a complete expert interview article with introduction, body, preface, and summary sections
Formats interview content in Q&A style with bold question headers and first-person expert answers
Processes existing interview draft content to create polished articles
Assembles all generated sections into a single markdown article
Produces articles up to 2000 words following specified formatting rules
Validates required inputs before generation to prevent incomplete outputs
limitations: Requires all inputs (background, questions, title) to be provided by the user
Cannot perform real interviews or interact with actual experts
Limited to text-based interview articles and cannot generate multimedia content
Output quality depends on the completeness of the provided expert background and questions

use_cases.title: Journalist preparing a publication-ready interview
use_cases.description: A journalist has conducted an interview and taken notes. They use this skill to transform their interview questions and expert background into a polished article with proper introduction, Q&A body, and summary.

use_cases.title: Medical editor formatting expert contributions
use_cases.description: A medical journal editor receives interview responses from a researcher. They use the skill to structure the content into a professional interview article with academic formatting.

use_cases.title: Researcher documenting expert insights for publication
use_cases.description: A researcher interviews a senior colleague about their work and uses the skill to create a well-structured interview article for a conference newsletter or institutional publication.

prompt_templates.title: Basic interview article from scratch
prompt_templates.prompt: I need help generating an expert interview article. Here is the expert background: [paste background]. Here are the interview questions: [paste questions]. The title is: [enter title]. Please generate the complete article following the workflow: introduction, Q&A body, preface, and summary.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with interview notes and expert profile ready

prompt_templates.title: Interview article with existing draft
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an existing interview draft that needs polishing. The expert background is: [paste background]. The questions are: [paste questions]. The draft content is: [paste draft]. The title is: [enter title]. Please refine the draft into a polished interview article with proper sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has rough interview notes that need professional formatting

prompt_templates.title: Academic interview with medical expert
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a formal academic interview article. Expert profile: Name: [name], Title: [title], Affiliation: [institution], Research Area: [area], Key Publications: [list]. Questions: [paste questions]. Title: [enter title]. Use formal academic language and include a technical preface explaining the research significance.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a publication-grade interview for an academic or medical journal

prompt_templates.title: Custom interview article with specific formatting
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an interview article with custom formatting. Background: [paste background]. Questions: [paste questions]. Title: [enter title]. Custom requirements: [specify formatting, tone, length, or section order]. Use the multi-step workflow but adapt the output format as specified.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user with specific formatting or structural requirements

output_examples.input: Expert background: Dr. Sarah Chen, Professor of Neuroscience at Stanford University, 15 years research in neuroplasticity, published 80+ papers. Questions: 1. What is neuroplasticity? 2. How does exercise affect the brain? 3. What are your current research directions? Title: The Plastic Brain: A Conversation with Dr. Sarah Chen
output_examples.output: A complete interview article with: (1) Expert profile section listing Dr. Chen's name, title, affiliation, and achievements; (2) Q&A body with bold questions and Dr. Chen's first-person answers; (3) 150-word preface introducing neuroplasticity research; (4) 150-word summary highlighting key insights from the interview

output_examples.input: Draft interview notes with incomplete sentences and informal language from a conversation with an AI researcher. Questions cover their background, current work, and predictions for the future of AI.
output_examples.output: A polished interview article with cleaned-up language, proper Q&A formatting, a preface contextualizing the AI field, a complete expert profile, and a summary of the key takeaways from the conversation

output_examples.input: Multiple expert profiles and a list of questions for a panel-style interview about climate change policy.
output_examples.output: A structured interview article featuring each expert's introduction followed by their responses to each question, with a preface on climate policy context and a summary synthesizing the different expert perspectives

best_practices: Provide detailed expert background including name, title, affiliation, and key achievements for a more accurate introduction section
Review and refine the generated Q&A content to ensure it accurately represents the expert's expected responses and maintains factual accuracy
Verify the final assembled article follows the intended structure and adjust section ordering or formatting as needed for your publication
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for live interview transcription or recording tasks, as it generates simulated content from provided inputs
Do not submit generated articles without reviewing for factual accuracy and consistency with the expert's actual views
Do not skip providing the expert background information, as this results in a generic and less useful introduction

faq.question: What inputs do I need to provide?
faq.answer: You need an expert background profile (name, title, affiliation, achievements), a list of interview questions, and an article title. Optionally, you can provide existing draft text.

faq.question: Does this skill conduct real interviews?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates a simulated interview article based on the inputs you provide. It does not call, email, or interact with real people.

faq.question: How long is the generated article?
faq.answer: The Q&A body targets approximately 2000 words. The preface and summary sections are approximately 150 words each. Total article length is typically 2500 to 3000 words.

faq.question: Can I use this for non-medical topics?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes medical-themed prompt examples but the workflow works for any topic where you have expert background information and interview questions.

faq.question: What format does the output use?
faq.answer: The output is a markdown article with sections for title, preface, expert profile, interview Q&A body, and summary.

faq.question: Can I edit parts of the generated article?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill generates each section separately. You can review and edit any section before the final assembly step.
